author: @Iwekzy
text:
Speaking of turning things into speakers, the arm from a physical harddisk will also play audible sound if you hook up a signal to the voice coil, while the arm will move with the power of the signal. Can be turned into a cool light show if you mount a mirror on the arm and shine a laser at it, or possible an sort of oscilliscope if you have two arms mounted as x and y.
Also the BLDC motor from HDD for the actual spinning is crazy fast, just sad that it's very hard to get it out of the metal enclosure on most of them.

author: @jamest.5001
text: They make a similar product here in the US, made of aluminum with a plastic in the center, to bend score with a saw cutting through only the inside of the bend, the aluminum only leaving the outside and middle intact, it's fairly thin aluminum, like aluminum flashing, something like 0.5mm, with the center about 2- 2.5mm thick, it's about 3-3.5mm total thickness, used for the same purpose, and making signs, it comes in large sheets,  it acts like 3mm thick aluminum only lighter, it's great stuff to make things with,

author: @CitizenSmith50
text: In Australia any form of expanded polythene material is prohibited in building work  "Effective 1 February 2021, the Minister for Planning announced a prohibition on the use of certain aluminium composite panels and expanded polystyrene for any building work in connection with class 2 – 9 buildings of Type A and Type B construction ",  so PVC board is not so cheap here.  It is mainly used for shop signage, and the retailers charge premium prices !

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: you really should do a video explaining the chemical reason that films and such like this do tighten up from the polymer chains being able to relax back to their original shape they were last in when fully liquid state, vs softened and stretched into a stressed state, especially since this stressed state offers that aspect of being a partial rectifier in clear options like cellophane, so two polarizing films on either side will generate a color rather than just getting darker or lighter on rotation :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good tip mate cheers

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I posted a video link to how the polarizer works with those polymer stretched chains in clear material like cellophane, between two pieces of the polarizing film from LCD monitors, (so salvaged too XD) the plastic is like a piece of the clear packaging over some popcorn too, if I recall, if not I know two layers of clear packing tape that is about 50mm or 2 inch wide works well too :)

author: @deslomeslager
text:
I think it may blow up the end-transistor of the amplifier, as it requires 4 to 8 Ohms. Maybe a good thing to tell viewers this might happen. And if they use the earphone outlet, it is even worse, that one expects an even higher impedance. Just saying .. .. ..
Having seen your previous video (the water lens), I thought you were going to make a speaker 'cone'. But I think a flat area is as good (for this application).
I did Google upvc, sintra and such, but it does not translate to Dutch very well. I did find PVC plates, but it is very expensive. Any Dutch people like to comment please, how to get it at a reasonable price?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well you could order from UK I am guessing it will still eb next day and not that expensive - anyhow have a look on the TnT channel when I do advise this

replies.author: @Alacritous
replies.text: Youtube is deleting this comment. I think because I'm mentioning a popular online retailer in China. This is the third time posting. There are things called "resonance speaker" on <censored name?> that you can buy. They turn any flat surface into a speaker cone. I have a set that I created a portable sound system out of. Two of the resonance speakers connected to an amplifier and battery in a small toolbox I got from Dollarama. I bought some double sided tacky adhesive to use to temporarily attach the speakers to something like a patio deck or picnic table or a wooden board or anything flat and play music from my phone through them and it's quite loud. The resonance speakers I bought claim to be 25 watt. Tech Ingredients made a video about these things and made some flat panel speakers out of them that perform as well as regular cone speakers. I'll post a link as a reply to this in case YouTube doesn't like it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it wouldn't have that much power I think mate - but give it a go - by the way all motors or indeed inductors work with this - they are cheap sound bugs really well worth experimenting with a few things if you feel like it

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are sound bugs I think mate - they use terfinol-D this is really a cheap version of a sound bug and uses the inductance of the motor - all inductors will do this
